California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Kirk Alan Pessner 
Paralegal Administrator 
Political Reports unit 
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson, 
Parrinello & Mueller 
July 24, 1989 
650 California street, suite 2650 
San Francisco, CA 
94108 
Dear Mr. Pessner: 
RE: 
Your Request for Informal 
Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-346 
You have requested confirmation of telephone assistance I 
provided to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of 
the Political Reform Act.ll 
Because you have not provided the 
name of your client, we consider your request to be for informal 
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed) .21 
Your letter, dated June 1, 1989, correctly summarizes my 
advice that ongoing independent expenditures made during the 17 
days prior to an election may be estimated and disclosed on a 
single late independent expenditure report. 
This advice is based 
on FPPC Advice Letter A-82-201 in which we advised it was 
permissible to estimate late contribution reports for non-monetary 
contributions of an ongoing nature. 
Please call me at (916)322-5662 if you have additonal 
questions. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
BY:~Kb-
Political Reform Consultant 
II Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the 
immunity provided by an 
or formal written 
ceo 
(Section 83114; Regulat 
29(c) (3).) 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 (I 
916) 322-5660 


SACRAMENTO 
770 L STREET, SUITE 800 
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752 
LAW OFFICES OF 
NIELSEN, MERKSAMER, 
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER 
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS 
650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650 
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108 
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800 
June 1, 1989 
Ms. Mary Ann Kvasager 
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division 
Fair Political Practices Commissi9n 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804-0807 
Re: 
Request for written Advice 
Dear Mary Ann: 
FILE NUMBER 
This letter will request written confirmation of oral 
advice you recently provided to me based on the following factual 
situation. 
A client of this firm has made independent expenditures 
in connection with a municipal election to be held June 6, 1989. 
The filer has already complied with the requirements to file the 
Supplemental Independent Expenditure Reports (FPPC Form 465) with 
the local jurisdiction on both pre-election campaign filing 
deadlines. 
The filer will continue to make independent 
expenditures, which will be in excess of $1,000, during the 
period between the closing date of the second pre-election 
campaign statement and election day (Government Code section 
82036.5) and intends to file a Late Independent Expenditure 
Report as required in Government Code Section 84203. 
We asked whether late independent expenditures during 
this period could be estimated and disclosed on a single Late 
Independent Expenditure Report in the same manner that the 
Commission staff permitted estimated Late Contribution Reports 
for non-monetary contributions of an ongoing nature. 
(FPPC 
Informal Advice Letter A-82-201) 
You indicated by telephone that 
such Late Independent Expenditure Reports could be filed in that 
same fashion, and I am requesting that you confirm that advice in 
writing. 


Ms. Mary Ann Kvasager 
June 1, 1989 
Page 2 
If you have any questions or need any additional 
information, please do not hesitate to contact me. 
KAP/mc 
Kind r

Poll. t.j.~/ eports Unl. t 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
June 7, 1989 
Kirk Alan Pessner 
Nielsen, Merksamer, Parrinello 
& Mueller 
650 California street, suite 2650 
San Francisco, CA 
94108 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-346 
Dear Mr. Pessner: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on June 5, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
Very truly you;:.s-r\

(916)322~5660
